title: EU steps up civil protection efforts and brings back 356 citizens home amid developments in the Middle East
content: 2 repatriation flights chartered directly by the European Commission have safely landed in Romania. The 2 flights brought back 356 European citizens who were stranded in the Middle East from Oman to Romania.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: Commission coordinates repatriation flights from the Middle East under the EU Civil Protection Mechanism
content: Yesterday and today, the European Commission's Emergency Response Coordination Centre (ERCC) has supported EU Member States with the organisation of 6 repatriation flights, bringing European citizens safely back to Bulgaria, Italy, Austria, and Slovakia.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: EU announces €63 million for Somalia to respond to the worsening humanitarian crisis
content: This announcement comes as drought related food insecurity and malnutrition rates are surging nationwide in Somalia, with 6.5 million people (i.e. around 1 in 3 Somalis) facing hunger and an estimated 1.85 million children under the age of 5 at risk of acute malnutrition.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: Sudan: joint statement on protection of civilians and humanitarian operations
content: Following the recent severe escalation in the conflict in Sudan, Commissioner Hadja Lahbib joined 32 countries to reiterate EU's full support to the people of Sudan and reaffirm its commitment to the respect of international humanitarian law.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: EU announces over €81 million in humanitarian aid as conflict in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo pushes Great Lakes crisis to new extremes
content: With this new humanitarian funding, the EU will provide life-saving services to the most vulnerable in the eastern DRC, but also to Congolese refugees forced to flee the country.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: Commissioner Lahbib visits Ukraine to reiterate EU support
content: While Russia continues to target Ukraine's energy infrastructure, leaving more than one million people without electricity and water, the European Union remains firmly at Ukraine's side to help its people endure another winter under attack.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: EU allocates over €123 million in humanitarian aid for Latin America and Caribbean in 2026
content: In a year of shrinking global aid, the EU is choosing solidarity, delivering life-saving aid and standing by international humanitarian law. Part of this aid will also bolster disaster preparedness and response, given the region's high exposure to natural hazards.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: EU allocates €63 million in response to the crisis in Myanmar and its impact on neighbouring countries
content: The European Commission has allocated €63 million in humanitarian aid to help populations affected by the ongoing conflict in Myanmar, as well as to support Rohingya refugees living in neighbouring countries, in particular in Bangladesh.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: EU announces €153 million in humanitarian aid to Ukraine and Moldova
content: The European Commission is announcing €153 million in emergency aid to Ukraine and refugee-hosting Moldova, as millions Ukrainians face freezing temperatures without power under sustained Russian bombardment of energy infrastructure.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: EU deploys emergency generators as Russian strikes leave 1 million Ukrainians without power in -20°C
content: The European Commission is today deploying 447 emergency generators from EU strategic reserves to restore power to hospitals, shelters and critical services. The generators - mobilised from rescEU strategic reserves hosted in Poland - will be distributed to the most affected communities.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: Commission announces €1.9 billion humanitarian aid budget for 2026
content: At a time when 239 million people need assistance and major donors are cutting funding. Commissioner Hadja Lahbib brings this commitment to Davos today, seeking to mobilise private sector finance and innovative solutions that can complement public funding and reach people in need.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: Statement by Commissioner Lahbib on the demolition of the UNRWA compound in East Jerusalem
content: The EU is committed to continuing its political and financial support to UNRWA. UNRWA's functioning contributes to maintaining conditions conducive to peace and security, and any disruption of its work has significant humanitarian, , political, and socio-economic repercussions.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: Commissioner Lahbib joins national commemoration in Switzerland for victims of Crans Montana fire
content: Commissioner for Crisis Management Hadja Lahbib is participating today in the official national commemoration honouring the victims of the Crans Montana fire and expressing solidarity with the injured, their families and the communities affected.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: Registration of international NGOs in Palestine: joint statement by High Representative Kallas and Commissioners Lahbib and Šuica
content: We call on Israel to allow international NGOs to operate and deliver life-saving aid to civilians in need in Palestine. Without these international NGOs, humanitarian aid cannot be delivered at the scale needed to prevent further loss of life in Gaza.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: EU stands in full solidarity with Switzerland and evacuates victims after Crans-Montana tragedy
content: The EU's Emergency Response Coordination Centre has immediately dispatched 2 liason officers to oversee the medical evacuation operation and liaise with the Swiss national authorities on the spot. The ERCC remains also in close contact, ready to coordinate other evacuations in the upcoming days.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: Commission delivers thermal power plant to supply power for 1 million Ukrainians
content: The European Commission has successfully relocated a full thermal power plant from Lithuania to Ukraine, completing its largest-ever coordinated logistical operation to date.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: EU boosts funding to protect aid workers with €4.35 million
content: This new funding will support humanitarian staff who experience critical incidents in the line of duty, ensuring their safety and recovery. The primary focus will be on local humanitarian workers as they are more exposed to armed violence and threats to their lives.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: ‘An unprecedented influx’: the EU responds to the Burundi–DR Congo border crisis
content: A renewed offensive by the M23 rebel group in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo’s South Kivu province has displaced around 500,000 people, and driven tens of thousands across the border into Burundi, overwhelming fragile transit sites and stretching already limited humanitarian resources.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: EU allocates €29 million for Myanmar and Rohingya humanitarian crises
content: These new funds come as the intensifying conflict in Myanmar continues to displace more people within the country, while also driving more displacement towards Bangladesh and neighbouring countries. The situation is further aggravated by the earthquake that hit Myanmar earlier this year.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: EU launches emergency air bridge to deliver aid into Darfur
content: A total of 8 flights will bring life-saving supplies into Darfur, as mass atrocities, starvation, and displacement linked to the conflict in Sudan have left millions in need of humanitarian assistance.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: EU steps up efforts to boost the efficiency of humanitarian aid through the strategic supply chain
content: Commissioner for Equality, Preparedness and Crisis Management, Hadja Lahbib participated in the Humanitarian Leadership Group on Supply Chain Conference and welcomed the agreement reached by participants to continue working for a more interoperable system to deliver humanitarian aid.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: EU releases €2.4 million flood response aid in Sri Lanka, Indonesia and Thailand
content: This funding will serve to provide essential relief to the most vulnerable communities.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: Joint statement by High Representative Kallas and Commissioners Šuica and Lahbib on the first anniversary of the fall of the Assad regime
content: After years of conflict and division, Syria faces immense challenges, but also tremendous opportunities, that can lead the country towards the brighter future its people envision and deserve.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: Europe faces worst wildfire year on record as fire seasons grow longer and more destructive
content: The high frequency and intensity of wildfires during prolonged fire seasons pose new challenges to firefighting services across Europe and globally. In response these challenges, the EU has recently strengthened its collective readiness by doubling the rescEU aerial fleet and pooling resources.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: EU scales up help after Pakistan floods with additional €3 million
content: The support will be delivered as cash assistance, so vulnerable household can meet immediate needs and start rebuilding their lives.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: Commissioner Lahbib travels to Egypt as EU pushes for unhindered Gaza relief
content: Commissioner for Equality, Preparedness, and Crisis Management, Hadja Lahbib, is in Egypt from 27 to 29 November to work with the Egyptian authorities and humanitarian partners on the ground on increasing the flow of humanitarian aid into Gaza.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: EU reinforces humanitarian response with €143 million for Sub-Saharan Africa
content: The emergency funding will support food assistance, water and sanitation, access to healthcare, and help the most vulnerable communities meet their basic needs.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News
